frames.name: Economic Impact/Market Analysis
frames.description: Focuses on financial market reactions, price movements, and economic consequences for different regions and countries
frames.emphasis: Oil price surges, regional vulnerability assessments, specific country impacts, and market data

frames.name: Geopolitical Crisis
frames.description: Frames the situation as an escalating military conflict with diplomatic implications
frames.emphasis: Military actions, casualties, international responses, and diplomatic calls for de-escalation

frames.name: Energy Security Threat
frames.description: Presents the story as a critical disruption to global energy infrastructure and supply chains
frames.emphasis: Supply chain vulnerabilities, infrastructure damage, and long-term energy security implications

frames.name: Historical Precedent Warning
frames.description: Emphasizes the unprecedented nature and potential scale of the disruption
frames.emphasis: Potential for 'historic' or 'epochal' market disruptions and lack of precedent for current situation

outlierPerspectives: China's specific diplomatic response calling for vessel protection (only in The Guardian)
Detailed casualty reporting including death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei (only in Inside Climate News)
U.S. consumer insulation from natural gas impacts but vulnerability to oil price increases (only in Inside Climate News)
Specific mention of fertilizer trade disruption through the strait (only in The Guardian)
missingContext: Legal basis or international law regarding strait closure
Historical precedents of Strait of Hormuz closures and their duration
Strategic petroleum reserve levels in affected countries
Alternative shipping routes and their capacity/costs
Timeline and specific triggers that led to the current escalation
Backup energy infrastructure and emergency protocols in affected regions
